scenario: Helios Retail authors a Camunda 8.8 fulfillment process that handles 5000 orders per day. The modeler reviews the BPMN coverage page to confirm which activity markers can be applied to subprocesses in production today, since the design relies on parallel multi-instance and compensation patterns inside the fulfillment subprocess.
question: Which activity marker is NOT shown as implemented in the Camunda 8.8 BPMN coverage markers table?

options.text: Multi-instance parallel marker is not marked implemented and cannot be applied to subprocesses in Camunda 8.8
Loop marker is not marked implemented and cannot be applied to subprocesses in Camunda 8.8
Compensation marker is not marked implemented and cannot be applied to subprocesses in Camunda 8.8
Ad-hoc marker is not marked implemented and cannot be applied to subprocesses in Camunda 8.8

a.text: Incorrect. The multi-instance parallel marker is rendered with the implemented class on the BPMN coverage markers table and links to the multi-instance subprocess documentation.

b.text: Correct. The loop marker on the BPMN coverage markers table has a placeholder link and is drawn without the implemented class, while multi-instance parallel, multi-instance sequential, compensation and ad-hoc markers are all implemented for Camunda 8.8.

c.text: Incorrect. The compensation marker appears on the BPMN coverage markers table with the implemented class and links to the compensation handler documentation page.

d.text: Incorrect. The ad-hoc marker is shown with the implemented class on the BPMN coverage markers table and links to the ad-hoc subprocesses documentation page.

explanation: Of the five markers in the Camunda 8.8 BPMN coverage markers table (multi-instance parallel, multi-instance sequential, loop, compensation, and ad-hoc), only the loop marker is rendered without the implemented class and therefore is not natively supported.
